The 'Potentially allowed backend' section lists backend/core/questionnaire/**/* as an edit target. THIS DIRECTORY DOES NOT EXIST. changes.citation: backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py lines 1645-1662: confidence_adjustments IS computed from LifestyleModifierEngine.apply() result, specifically from engine_result['adjusted_system_burdens'][s]['confidence_penalty'] per system. Written into lifestyle_artifact and stored in AnalysisResult.lifestyle field (backend/core/models/results.py line 283). The zero-output finding from prior audit is a registry/input activation issue, not an absent computation path. Scope A computation trace must verify whether effective_lifestyle_inputs reach the engine block.

changes.detail: No competing LC-S13 prompt exists outside latest_cursor_prompt.md. No duplicate lifestyle or coherence authority source detected.

Intelligence Core components in scope explicitly identified: (1) backend/core/analytics/lifestyle_modifier_engine.py — adjusts system burden vector; (2) backend/core/analytics/lifestyle_interpretation_bridge_engine.py — fires lifestyle bridges; (3) backend/core/analytics/lifestyle_registry_loader.py — loads SSOT registry into engine; (4) backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py — calls engine, builds lifestyle_artifact, writes results.lifestyle; (5) backend/core/pipeline/questionnaire_mapper.py — maps questionnaire inputs to lifestyle factors; (6) Sentinel packs — deterministic guard layer. All HIGH-risk per SOP §10.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Q-1/Q-2 questionnaire dependency — MATERIAL STOP RISK
changes.detail: Multiple active questionnaire branches detected in repo: feature/questionnaire-ux-redesign, feature/questionnaire-visual-redesign, feature/questionnaire-schema-wiring, feature/questionnaire-recovery, wp3/questionnaire-rationalisation. CLAUDE.md confirms Q-1/Q-2 redesign is 'in progress'. The prompt's Q-1/Q-2 dependency check is correctly structured (three explicit outcomes). This is a high-probability STOP trigger for Scope A if questionnaire input shape is actively changing. Cursor must complete the Q-1/Q-2 check before touching any questionnaire/lifestyle code and must STOP if outcome 3 applies.

Three scopes explicitly defined: Scope A (lifestyle propagation — trace-first, STOP/split if computation broken), Scope B (coherence guards — deterministic tests + Sentinel), Scope C (narrative language audit — classify and remediate overclaim). Sprint is correctly prohibited from touching: unit conversion, scoring directionality, Knowledge Bus content, SSOT metadata, Automation Bus scripts. No scope creep detected.

changes.check: Stage 2B — STOP/split condition for Scope A
changes.detail: Prompt correctly requires trace-first before wiring. If confidence_adjustments are not producing meaningful output, Scope A must STOP. Proposed split into LC-S13A (repair) and LC-S13B (surface) is correctly marked as requiring GPT/human authorisation. Scopes B and C may continue independently only if GPT/human approve.

Cross-sprint guard preflight explicitly requires: LC-S8F, LC-S8G, LC-S8D, LC-S10B, LC-S11A regression suites before implementation. New LC-S13 test target named: test_lc_s13_lifestyle_coherence_narrative.py. Homocysteine lead finding, uploaded-panel fidelity, and frontend conversion maths absence preserved as regression expectations.

Sprint must not change measured biomarker values, override lab-derived ranges, or introduce frontend conversion maths. Lifestyle modifiers apply only as additive adjustments to system burden vector — not to biomarker truth. Narrative audit scope correctly bounded to overclaim detection, not clinical interpretation. Determinism policy preserved.

Sentinel update explicitly required. Six defect classes specified: lifestyle_visible_payoff_missing, lifestyle_bridge_internal_code_leakage, domain_band_headline_polarity_contradiction, domain_active_signal_false_claim, mock_mode_personalisation_overclaim, governance_label_user_visible_leakage. Each must point to an active deterministic regression test. Placeholder entries are prohibited.

LC-S12B audit required Gate A three-party approval (Claude Code PASS, GPT Architecture, Human product owner) before LC-S13. User's request to harden LC-S13 implies Gate A is approved. No structural prompt issue. Noted for standing record.

changes.citation: docs/audit-papers/LC-S13_lifestyle_coherence_narrative_notes.md does not yet exist. All 12 required items in the implementation note are clearly specified. Output file does not conflict with any existing file.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. All primary authority paths confirmed present. All regression test paths confirmed present. risk_level HIGH and change_type MIXED correctly classified. STOP/split logic for Scope A is well-structured. Two path discrepancies in the 'Potentially allowed backend' section are recorded as warnings: backend/core/lifestyle/ and backend/core/questionnaire/ do not exist — Cursor must search for actual code in backend/core/analytics/ and backend/core/pipeline/ respectively, and must not create these directories. Q-1/Q-2 active branches are a high-probability STOP trigger; prompt's dependency check correctly handles this. confidence_adjustments computation is confirmed active in orchestrator.py (lines 1645-1662); zero-output is an input/registry activation issue, not an absent path. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start once the two path warnings are acknowledged.
